page: Amazon Web Services m9g.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 architecture using AWS Neoverse-V3 cores running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 381,353.24 and a PassMark CPU score of 107,221.26. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching top-tier speeds of 27,574.84 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 729.22 GB/sec for large-block reads. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the average tier.

The instance exhibits strong capabilities in database operations, achieving 507.16 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and top-tier PassMark database performance. However, there are notable performance tradeoffs in specific workloads; while LLM prompt processing for a 7B model is top-tier at 368.62 tokens per second, text generation speeds for smaller models fall into the poor or average tiers. Given its high core density, balanced memory ratio, and 100 Gbps network baseline, this instance is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, large-scale web serving, database operations, and parallel data decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated physical ARMv9 cores powered by AWS Neoverse-V3 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 100 Gbps baseline network speed. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database operation speeds, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to poor. Qualitatively, the high physical core density and balanced memory allocation offer excellent resource density for scale-out workloads without hyperthreading overhead. This resource profile makes the m9g.48xlarge highly efficient for high-density multi-threaded applications, Redis deployments, static web serving, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated ARMv9 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARMv9 general-purpose platform featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 768 GB of RAM for highly parallelized enterprise workloads.

768.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Suited for Redis deployments, static web serving, and parallel decompression
